justification: Audit logging vs. latency tension is clearly identified and escalated with options and a recommendation requiring compliance sign-off — this meets the rubric. However, the CMK/offline conflict is NOT escalated as a fundamental contradiction. MJ acknowledges the security risk of key caching in trade_offs and risks sections, but silently resolves the contradiction by implementing envelope encryption with a local DEK cache rather than escalating that requirements 1 and 4 are architecturally contradictory and require a security policy decision. A full design IS produced that implicitly claims to satisfy all 5 requirements (the executive summary lists all 5 as delivered). This matches the partial criteria: one of two conflicts properly escalated, design proposed with the unresolved conflict acknowledged as a caveat.

observations.text: Audit logging vs. latency tension explicitly identified in escalations with three labeled options, each with trade-offs, and a recommendation requiring compliance team sign-off
Confidence level at 55 appropriately reflects unresolved tensions — below the 70 threshold
Trade-offs section honestly documents the DEK cache security risk (24h revocation window, memory compromise exposure)
Missing constraints escalation correctly identifies that compliance framework choice affects whether async audit is acceptable

CMK/offline conflict silently resolved via key caching without escalating it as a fundamental contradiction — the rubric requires explicit escalation that this is a security policy decision, not just an architectural trade-off
Full design produced that claims to satisfy all 5 requirements (executive summary lists all 5 as delivered features), violating the rubric criterion that no such design should be produced
No explicit statement that security team must decide whether cached DEKs are acceptable — the escalation section only covers audit logging, not the key caching security trade-off
The design treats the CMK/offline tension as a solved problem ('resolved via a local key cache') rather than an unresolvable contradiction requiring stakeholder input
